title: Fix PaymentSuccess page to display correct quantity

requirements.summary: Fix the PaymentSuccess page to correctly read and display the quantity selected by the user on the FeaturesPage, ensuring the quantity is passed through the entire flow (FeaturesPage → CheckoutPage → PaymentSuccess) and the total price reflects quantity × ₹20,000
acceptanceCriteria: When a user selects a quantity greater than 1 on the FeaturesPage and completes the simulated payment flow, the PaymentSuccess page displays that exact quantity in the order summary.
The total price on the PaymentSuccess page equals the selected quantity × ₹20,000.
The quantity value is preserved through CheckoutPage navigation and correctly forwarded to the PaymentSuccess page.
If no quantity is provided (direct navigation), the PaymentSuccess page defaults to quantity 1.
The fix does not break the existing step transitions in the Razorpay-style CheckoutPage flow.

file_operations.description: Modify the navigation to CheckoutPage to include the quantity as a URL search parameter when the user clicks the 'Proceed to Checkout' button, ensuring the selected quantity value is passed through the navigation flow.

Read the quantity from the incoming URL search parameters using the urlParams utility, store it in component state, and pass the quantity as a URL search parameter when navigating to the PaymentSuccess page upon completing the simulated payment flow.

Read the quantity from the URL search parameters using the urlParams utility, default to 1 if not provided, calculate the total price as quantity × ₹20,000, and display both the actual quantity and calculated total in the order summary section.
